channel.title: US Top News and Analysis
channel.description: CNBC is the world leader in business news and real-time financial market coverage. Find fast, actionable information.

item.title: Mag 7 value shrinks by $2.3 trillion amid AI spending jitters — but investors are still backing chipmakers
item.description: Investors are growing jittery about massive AI spending by the Mag 7 and when these companies will produce a return on that investment.

item.title: Oil prices set for steep monthly drop as Trump, Iran issue mixed messages on talks in Qatar
item.description: The moves come as oil traders monitor the prospect of U.S.-Iran talks in Doha on Tuesday.

item.title: Medicare will start covering obesity drugs for the first time. Here's what patients should know
item.description: The move could unlock millions of new patients for Novo Nordisk and Eli Lilly and expand access to medications that were previously out of reach for seniors.

item.title: Stocks making the biggest moves premarket: AeroVironment, Strategy, Merck & more
item.description: These are the stocks posting the largest moves premarket.

item.title: Comcast is spinning out its media business. Deutsche Bank says it's time to buy the stock
item.description: Comcast is planning to spin off NBCUniversal, which makes the stock more attractive to own, according to Deutsche Bank.

item.title: Do you really, really love your job? Then you're not alone, according to surprising results from this survey
item.description: Those surveyed showed a 78.9% rate of workers who "reported feeling positive at the end of their shifts."

item.title: Apple iPhone 18 Pro supplier list, parts and photos exposed in Tata data leak
item.description: The exposure threatens the carefully negotiated business of building the iPhone, which Apple assembles from a thicket of suppliers worldwide.

item.title: French biotech stock soars 34% after new data on experimental medicine
item.description: Shares of French biotech company Abivax soared Tuesday after it released fresh data on its lead drug for bowel disease.

item.title: Waymo and Uber end robotaxi pilot in Phoenix
item.description: The Waymo self-driving cars deployed in Phoenix for the Uber pilot will remain in use and make autonomous deliveries with DoorDash.

item.title: AeroVironment soars 19% on earnings beat, backlog grows to $1.2 billion
item.description: Dronemaker AeroVironment's autonomous system revenue beat expectations by $90 million and its backlog continued to grow.

item.title: Buffett delays annual donation to Gates Foundation pending review of Jeffrey Epstein ties: WSJ
item.description: This comes as the foundation continues to review its ties to the late sex offender Jeffery Epstein.

item.title: Supreme Court rulings on Fed, FTC: What they mean for consumers
item.description: The Supreme Court issued a pair of highly anticipated rulings on Monday that have far-reaching implications for household finances.

item.title: Rafael Nadal talks tennis prize money, his hotels, and what sports taught him about business
item.description: Rafael Nadal opened his fourth Zel hotel as he expands into hospitality, education and sports after retiring from tennis.

item.title: Japanese yen sinks to 40-year low, keeping intervention risks in focus
item.description: The Japanese yen weakened to its lowest level against the U.S. dollar since 1986 on Tuesday, keeping investors on alert for possible intervention from Japanese authorities.

item.title: Comcast's NBCUniversal spinoff raises hope for more deals. There may not be good options
item.description: Comcast plans to separate its cable and media divisions over the next year. This could set each company up for M&A, but there might not be any good options.

item.title: Short sellers keep betting against Pop Mart — even though it's been a losing trade
item.description: Short sellers are doubling down on Pop Mart International even as a recent share price recovery turns their bearish bets on the Chinese toymaker increasingly risky.

item.title: Here's how Trump Accounts could affect women's retirement savings gap
item.description: While Trump Accounts are unlikely to directly improve retirement savings among women, there may be an indirect benefit, says one expert.

item.title: Markets should beware 'pain trade' shocks in the second half: HSBC
item.description: Markets should brace for "pain trades" in the second half of this year, including a steepening U.S. Treasury, unabated AI trade and an "explosive" U.S. dollar, according to an HSBC report.

item.title: Digital Realty falls 5% after taking $3.5 billion stake in Blackstone's Virginia data centers
item.description: Digital Realty fell in premarket trading Tuesday after it announced its buying a $3.5 billion stake in three data centers from asset manager Blackstone.

item.title: Salesforce is on an AI buying spree, but Wall Street still has its doubts
item.description: The company has announced three deals in the month of June alone.

item.title: Eli Lilly, Regeneron among first companies selected for FDA initiative to speed review of new manufacturing facilities
item.description: Eli Lilly and Regeneron are among the first seven companies the FDA has selected for its PreCheck Pilot Program, CNBC has learned.

item.title: What are VA rehab and renovation loans — plus the best lenders
item.description: People who have served in the military could get up to 100% financing to buy and rehab a fixer-upper.

item.title: Indonesia jails former education minister for 10 years in Google Chromebook graft case
item.description: Makarim was found guilty in a case over the procurement of Google Chromebooks for schools

item.title: Industry lobbyists push House committee to block a ban on Pentagon defense contractors buying back their own stock
item.description: Defense companies and their trade groups are pressing Congress not to require that the Pentagon approve stock buybacks.

item.title: Inflation likely to stay ‘significantly above target,’ top European central banker warns
item.description: Joachim Nagel, president of Germany’s Bundesbank, spoke to CNBC on the sidelines of an ECB conference in Portugal.

item.title: Apple supplier Luxshare seeks up to $3.1 billion in Hong Kong share sale
item.description: Apple supplier Luxshare Precision Industry is seeking to raise as much as $24.27 billion Hong Kong dollars ($3.1 billion) through a Hong Kong share sale.

item.title: China factory activity grows faster than expected in June on tech export demand
item.description: China's manufacturing engine held up this year as surging demand for artificial intelligence technology helped offset the drag from Middle East turmoil.

item.title: Trump bought as much as $5 million in Axon stock before ICE sought $220 million Taser deal
item.description: Axon Enterprise has ramped up lobbying in Congress on federal law enforcement technology as ICE pursues a potential $220 million Taser deal.

item.title: Australia's competition regulator takes Amazon to court over alleged unfair Prime subscription contract terms
item.description: Australia's competition regulator is taking Amazon to court, alleging its Prime contracts required subscribers to pay AU$2.99 to avoid advertising, with no option for refunds

item.title: Supreme Court rules Trump cannot fire Fed Governor Lisa Cook for now
item.description: The ruling by the Supreme Court means that Lisa Cook will remain as a Federal Reserve governor as her lawsuit challenging Trump's effort to fire her proceeds.
